title: Achariterium
taxonomy: Achariterium is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Annonaceae. This family is commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The Annonaceae family consists of around 130 genera and over 2,000 species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Achariterium is one of the lesser-known genera within this family, but its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other Annonaceae species. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and characteristics of the Achariterium genus.
